issue_title: JPA Map store JDBC transactions not rolled back on Hibernate transaction rollback when running in Quarkus
issue_body:
### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Given a setup with Quarkus and JPA map storage, a transaction is not rolled back. Instead, the changes are written to the database.


### Version

19.0.2

### Expected behavior

When a transaction is marked for rollback, it should be rolled back.


### Actual behavior

When the rollback is triggered to the Hibernate transaction, that is not propagated to the underlying PostgreSQL DB connection. 
Instead, when the DB connection is returned, it is being committed.


### How to Reproduce?

* Start Quarkus as described in discussion #9596 with JPA map storage
* Login and select a client
* Change the “Valid redirect URIs” to contain a “#text” (an anchor) at the end
* Press “Save” -> an error message appears
* Press reload in the browser to load the changes from the database -> the changes have been persisted to the database


### Anything else?

Part of the cause might be that Hibernate is still using JTA for transactions, but the JDBC connection is not bound to the JTA transaction; instead it needs to be handled manually.

Possible remedies: 
* Manually commit/rollback the JDBC transaction in JpaTransactionWrapper with something like: 
em.unwrap(SessionImpl.class).doWork(Connection::rollback);
* Use JTA all the way for JPA Map storage in Quarkus 
* Use RESOURCE_LOCAL transaction type
commit_message:
Use RESOURCE_LOCAL transactions for JPA map storage

Closes #15248

issue_title: Disabling hostname strict in prod doesn't disable https
issue_body:
### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Disabling `hostname-strict` in prod mode still results in URLs with https when running locally, which is causing the admin console to not load.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

With `hostname-strict=false` it is expected that URLs are fully dynamic, but they still switch to https.

### Actual behavior

It seems the hostname is not fully dynamic with only `hostname-strict=false`, and they still use URLs.

### How to Reproduce?

Run Keycloak in prod mode locally using:

```
bin/kc.sh start --http-enabled=true --hostname-strict=false
```

Then check http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration, and observe:

```
"issuer":"https://localhost:8443/realms/master"
```

As hostname strict is disabled it should be using dynamic resolution of the URL, but this is not fully the case, only parts of the URL is dynamic.

Now run with:

```
bin/kc.sh start --http-enabled=true --hostname-strict=false --hostname-strict-https=false
```

This time around http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration is using http URLs:

```
"issuer":"http://localhost:8080/realms/master"
```

However, it shouldn't be necessary to include `hostname-strict-https=false` in this case, in fact this is actually slightly worse since it's an internal option that is not documented.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Disabling strict https if hostname is diasabled

Closes #15287

issue_title: Client-scoped roles are not assignable when using admin fine-grained permissions
issue_body:
### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

We're experimenting with the preview feature admin fine-grained permissions. We've been following the official [documentation ](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_fine_grain_permissions). Overall, everything seems to work well.

We've been able to set up a user that's only allowed to manage a client, assign realm roles, and group memberships (among others).

We are however having issues getting the client-scoped role mapping assignment permission to work. We've tried both the fine-grained role permissions as described in the [documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_fine_grain_permissions), and the client-wide `map-roles` permission.

The user we're testing with has the `view-users` role and is able to navigate to the `Role mapping` tab for any user. We're also able to click the `Assign role` button. However, we're unable to list client-scoped roles associated with clients for which we applied a user policy to the `map-roles` permission.

We do see a call going out to the following URL but no results are returned:

```
https://localhost:9000/auth/admin/realms/test/admin-ui-available-roles/users/f12b1055-bcc6-4c85-b38b-825876452dfa?first=0&max=11&search=
```

![no-roles-to-assign](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5998869/199542097-aa1f35e8-59e3-44a1-aa67-036962fa76aa.gif)

One interesting observation is that when a regular admin user would assign a client-scoped role to our dummy user, the user that was granted the `map-roles` permission is able to unassign that role.

![unassign-role](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5998869/199543319-0aa7c971-9db4-4bef-96b5-d73079b55faa.gif)


### Version

19.0.3 and 20.0.0

### Expected behavior

A user that has the `map-roles` permission (either on an individual client-scoped role or on an entire client) should be able to assign client-scoped roles.

### Actual behavior

No client-scoped roles are listed in the `Assign role` dialog

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the official [documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#restrict-user-role-mapping).

### Anything else?

_No response_

fixed fine grain permissions (#16668)

fixes: #3700

issue_title: No email send on TOTP/Authenticator app removal
issue_body:
### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

When a TOTP device/authenticator app is removed via account console, no email is send via the email event listener.
This worked with the old account console, but does not with the new account console.

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

An email should be send to the user that notifies the user about the removal of TOTP device/Authenticator app.

### Actual behavior

No email is send when the new account console is used.
With the old account console the email is send.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a file named `docker-compose.yml` with this content:
```yaml
version: '3'
services:
  keycloak:
    container_name: keycloak
    image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:20.0.1
    environment:
      KC_SPI_EVENTS_LISTENER_EMAIL_ENABLED: "true"
      KC_SPI_EVENTS_LISTENER_EMAIL_INCLUDE_EVENTS: UPDATE_TOTP,REMOVE_TOTP
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: admin
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: admin
      DEBUG_PORT: '*:8787'
      DEBUG: 'true'
    command: ['start-dev', '--debug']
    ports:
      - 8080:8080
      - 8787:8787
  mail:
    container_name: mail
    image: maildev/maildev
    ports:
      - 1080:1080

```

- Start the docker containers via `docker-compose up`.
- Navigate to http://localhost:8080/admin/master/console/#/
- Login with user `admin` and password `admin`
- Navigate to `Users -> admin`
  - Add `admin@local.local` as email address
  - Mark email as verified
  - Save the changes
- Navigate to `Realm settings -> Email`
  - Add `keycloak@local.local` as from address
  - Add `mail` as SMTP host
  - Add `1025` as SMTP port
  - Save the changes
  - Test connection (should succeed)
- Navigate to `Realm settings -> Events -> Event Listeners`
  - Add `email` to the list of event listeners
  - Save changes
- Navigate to http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/#/security/signingin
  - Click `Setup authenticator application`
  - Register authenticator app via barcode
- Navigate to http://localhost:1080/#/
  - You should see an email with subject `Update OTP`
- Navigate http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/#/security/signingin
  - Remove the authenticator application
- Navigate to http://localhost:1080/#/
  - refresh the page
  - ⚠️ No email with subject `Remove OTP` is shown
- In the admin console navigate to `Realm settings -> themes` (http://localhost:8080/admin/master/console/#/master/realm-settings/themes)
  - Change the `Account theme` to `keycloak` (old account management console)
  - Save changes
- Navigate to http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/totp
  - Register authenticator app via barcode
 - Navigate to http://localhost:1080/#/
  - refresh the page
  - An additional email with subject `Update OTP` is shown
- Navigate to http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/totp
  - Delete the configured authenticator
- Navigate to http://localhost:1080/#/
  - refresh the page
  - ✔️ Email with subject `Remove OTP` is shown

### Anything else?

The old account console uses this endpoint to delte the authenticator app: `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/totp`
The new account console uses this endpoint instead: `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/credentials/<credential_id>`

It looks like the credential endpoint does not fire the `REMOVE_TOTP` event.

issue_title: User Federation: getReadable() can throw a NPR for a federated user if the user has no attributes
issue_body:
### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

for some imported users  loaded from a federated storage source (and that have no attributes), the Attributes class throws an NPE here https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/Attributes.java#L141

This happens if the LegacyAttributes are the provided implementation. This is default implementation unless declarative user profile is used ( declarative user profile is considered as an experiemental feature)

here to be more precise: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/LegacyAttributes.java#L49

This leads to an NPE and also the user details page in the admin console cannot be loaded 



### Version

20.0.0

### Expected behavior

No error is thrown even if the federated users have empty attributes map 

I think it's better to return an empty map rather than null to avoid NPE and also not having to depend on a feature activated

### Actual behavior

NPE is thrown, and the following message is shown 


![localhost_8080_auth_admin_master_console_ (2)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6265563/201541912-2ba65acf-eaa5-41da-8895-50b216fae13a.png)




### How to Reproduce?

This requires creating a custom user provider that implements `UserLookupProvider`

if the UserModel returned by either getUserById, getUserByUsername, getUserByEmail has empty attributes map which is possible because not all users have attributes, you will see an error in the admin console when navigating to the user details in the users admin section 



### Anything else?

This can be avoided by activating the declartive user profile (-Dkeycloak.profile.feature.declarative_user_profile=enabled) which uses the 

DefaultAttributes (https://github.dev/keycloak/keycloak/blob/009ca27a387146ac24e0f82a8e2919af5fe026b4/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/DefaultAttributes.java#L1) as an implementation. 

However the declarative user profile is only experimental for now.

authentication

### Version

many users can get an access token at the same time (X.509 authentication)

### How to Reproduce?

 ```
/* load cert */
  private def getKeyStore(jksFile : File): KeyManagerFactory = {
```

issue_title: Able to modify built-in flow
issue_body:
### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

I am able to add a sub-flow to a built-in flow (e.g. registration). See bottom of screenshot:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/97203210/202324055-5ee7e703-5a57-4f92-8ca7-ce7509601973.png)




### Version

20.0.0

### Expected behavior

Since I am unable to add steps to a "Built-in" flow (resulting in "Could not update flow: It is illegal to add execution to a built in flow"), I assume the built-in flows are meant to be immutable. 

Therefore, I would not expect to be able to add a sub-flow.



### Actual behavior

I am able to add a sub-flow to a built-in flow.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Go to Authentication in admin UI.
2. Select "registration (Built-in)" flow.
3. Click "Add sub-flow"
4. Leave the defaults with the exception of providing a required name.
5. Click "Add"

### Anything else?

_No response_

Do not allow add sub-flow to built-in workflow

issue_title: Display a duplicate user when we use ldap federation
issue_body:
### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

1 - Configure ldap provider to use federation user (openldap)
    Synchronization settings
       Import users  Off
       Sync Registrations Off
   Batch size 
       Periodic full sync Off
       Periodic changed users sync  Off
2 - When i create the first user in my ldap. the search user in keycloak UI return duplicate user (the same use)  ==> KO
3 - When i create the seconde user in my ldap the search user in keycloak UI return only one (not duplicate) ==> OK


### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

We need get only one result for the first user creation in ldap

### Actual behavior

returned two result for the first user creation in ldap

### How to Reproduce?

1 - Configure ldap provider to use federation user (openldap)
    Synchronization settings
       Import users  Off
       Sync Registrations Off
   Batch size 
       Periodic full sync Off
       Periodic changed users sync  Off
2 - When i create the first user in my ldap. the search user in keycloak UI return duplicate user (the same use)  ==> KO
3 - When i create the seconde user in my ldap the search user in keycloak UI return only one (not duplicate) ==> OK


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Keycloak wrongly assumes that the default datasource is the first one
issue_body:
### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Keycloak assumes, at least at the following places:
- [org.keycloak.quarkus.deployment.KeycloakProcessor, line 323](/keycloak/keycloak/blob/28fc5b4574ed5c8cc000ae11d5595d91af7f6a70/quarkus/deployment/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/deployment/KeycloakProcessor.java#L323)
- [org.keycloak.quarkus.deployment.LiquibaseProcessor, line  46](/keycloak/keycloak/blob/28fc5b4574ed5c8cc000ae11d5595d91af7f6a70/quarkus/deployment/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/deployment/LiquibaseProcessor.java#L46)

that the first datasource is the _default_ datasource.

While this assumption is correct for the `List<AggregatedDataSourceBuildTimeConfigBuildItem>` as produced by [`io.quarkus.agroal.deployment.AgroalProcessor.getAggregatedConfigBuildItems()`](/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/a2e7f7e8502b0e730083a301892e5a26251393c7/extensions/agroal/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/agroal/deployment/AgroalProcessor.java#L279-L325), it is <ins>wrong</ins> for the `List<JdbcDataSourceBuildItem>` as produced by [`io.quarkus.agroal.deployment.AgroalProcessor.generateDataSourceBeans()`](/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/a2e7f7e8502b0e730083a301892e5a26251393c7/extensions/agroal/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/agroal/deployment/AgroalProcessor.java#L229-L277). Notice that this method produces the datasources in the order determined by the iteration order of the `HashMap` returned by [`io.quarkus.agroal.deployment.AgroalProcessor.getDataSourceSupport()`](/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/a2e7f7e8502b0e730083a301892e5a26251393c7/extensions/agroal/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/agroal/deployment/AgroalProcessor.java#L179-L193).

This wrong assumption leads to problems, such as those described in issue #14353, under the following circumstances:
- there is an additional datasource defined for Keycloak
- the database kind of the additional datasource is different from the database kind of the default datasource
- the name of the additional datasource happens to come before the name of the default datasource - `<default>` - as determined by the java's `HashMap` iteration order (an example of such a name is `test`; on the other hand, `atest` is OK, as it comes _after_ the `<default>`)

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Defining an additional datasource has no negative impact in Keycloak.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak fails to start, reporting errors as described in issue #14353.

### How to Reproduce?

1. define an adittional datasource for Keycloak, for example in `application.properties`, as follows:
    ```properties
    quarkus.datasource.test.db-kind=oracle
    quarkus.datasource.test.jdbc.url=${DB_URL}
    quarkus.datasource.test.username=${DB_USER}
    quarkus.datasource.test.password=${DB_PW}
    ```
2. make sure that:
   * the DB kind of the datasource is different from the DB kind of the Keycloak's default datasource
   * the name of the datasource comes before `<default>` as determined by the java's `HashMap` iteration order (`test` does)
3. start Keycloak

### Anything else?

There is a workaround for this problem: Rename the aditional datasource to anything which comes _after_ the `<default>` as determined by the java's `HashMap` (or `HashSet`) iteration order - for example `atest`.

Keycloak wrongly assumes that the default datasource is the first one

issue_title: Client registration service must not check client protocol for Bearer token
issue_body:
### Description

According to Keycloak documentation for [5,1,1 Bearer token](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/securing_apps/index.html#authentication), bearer token can used for Client Registration.

However, SAML Clients can not be created with bearer token because Keycloak check for protocol in azp claim of bearer token. It is not logic. SAML Client must be registered if user has at least one of create-client or manage-client admin roles.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Being able to create SAML Clients with Client registration.

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: Fix update of group mappers on certain changes of the group path
issue_body:
### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

In context of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/13394, I have implemented an update mechanism for group mappers (currently there is only "Advanced Claim to Group"). This mechanism should update a mapper's group reference, whenever a group's path changes (due to group rename or move).

However, the mechanism does work in some cases, as further described below.

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

The mapper's group reference should be updated in the following cases (amongst other already implemented cases):

1. group rename: when an ancestor group is renamed
2. group move: when a group is converted from subgroup to top-level or when a subgroup's parent is changed

### Actual behavior

For case 1 mentioned above, the group reference is NOT updated at all.
For case 2, when using MapRealmProvider/MapGroupProvider, the group reference is updated as expected. For JpaRealmProvider, the group reference is NOT updated.

### How to Reproduce?

See new tests in the linked PR.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix update of group mappers on certain changes of the group path

The group reference in the mapper was not updated in the following cases:
- group rename: when an ancestor group was renamed
- (only for JpaRealmProvider, NOT for MapRealmProvider/MapGroupProvider) group move: when a group was converted from subgroup to top-level or when a subgroup's parent was changed

Closes #15614

issue_title: UserInfo: Role name mapper is not respected for user info endpoint
issue_body: ### Area core ### Describe the bug When enabling the "Add to userinfo" in the realm roles mapper like this The realm roles are added to the user info endpoint as expected. We then configured a "Role Name Mapper" for certain roles to rename them (we need this for software integration, where certain role names are expected). We did this like this ### Version 9.x.x ### Expected behavior We should expect that we have a role named "new_role" in the response of the user info endpoint. ### Actual behavior We have the original role named "old_role". For access token and id token it works as expected but not for the user info endpoint. ### How to Reproduce? See description. ### Anything else? I have tested this bug in version 18.x.x with the same result. Even when the OIDC spec doesn't define how to represent roles and groups specifically it states that claims have to be returned. This implies that a claim in a token like (realm_access.roles) should be represented exactly the same in the user info endpoint, I would guess. https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#UserInfo

issue_title: oidc - JavaScript-Adapter LocalStorage#clearExpired does not clear all possible items
issue_body:
### Area

adapter/javascript

### Describe the bug

It does not really cause a huge problem but the code in `LocalStorage#clearExpired` looks suspicous because if keys who start with 'kc-callback-' are next to each other only every 2nd will be cleared.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/28fc5b4574ed5c8cc000ae11d5595d91af7f6a70/adapters/oidc/js/src/keycloak.js#L1622-L1640

If you look at the code you see it walks the local-storage and removes if the key starts with 'kc-callback-' but as `i` is advanced everytime and hence always the key exactly after the removed is missed. 

It is not a big problem because it will be simply cleared on the next call.

I have no reproducer for `keycloak.js` but the effect can be seen in browser dev-tools if you execute the code below:

```javascript
localStorage.length // prints 0
localStorage.setItem('kc-callback-a', 'a'); 
localStorage.setItem('kc-callback-b', 'b'); 
for( let i = 0; i < localStorage.length; i++ ) { if( localStorage.key(i).startsWith('kc-callback-') ) { localStorage.removeItem(localStorage.key(i)) } } 
localStorage.length  // prints 1 but it would be expected to be 0
```

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

I think the expected behavior is that the cache is clearExpired clears all possible entries and does not leave entries who could have been removed.

### Actual behavior

Not all possible entries are removed.

### How to Reproduce?

see bug description

### Anything else?

The simplest possible solution is to reverse the loop

```javascript
for (var i = 0; i < localStorage.length; i++)  {
```
to
```javascript
for (var i = localStorage.length - 1; i >= 0; i--)  {
```

Clear all entries stored in local storage (#34056)

Closes #15635

issue_title: Internal pipelines don't tests run for OracleDB or other database
issue_body:
### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

The internal pipelines have been switch to run Quarkus. Apparently the fix in #13642 hasn't been incomplete, and when the tests run on `db-allocator` the `keycloak.storage.connections.vendor` is not set, so the DB settings forwarded to the Quarkus CLI.

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Tests should run on Oracle and all other databases.

### Actual behavior

Tests run on H2 all the time.

### How to Reproduce?

Have a look at the CLIs created in the internal pipelines.

issue_title: Review `set-quarkus-version.sh`
issue_body:
We should review/reconsider the following:
* Use of `mvn versions:revert`.
   * This is sometimes causing issues like e.g. [re-introducing previously removed properties](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/15601).
* Updates of dependencies only in `quarkus` module.
   * We should be probably also updating the dependencies in the root POM.

Use git checkout to rever changes

issue_title: No localization support for consent label 'Has access to' on account v2 applications page
issue_body:
### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

The account ApplicationsPage.tsx contains texts that are not wrapped in a Msg.localize operation. It is therefore impossible to have them translated into different languages.

e.g.  <DescriptionListTerm>Has access to</DescriptionListTerm>
Should be
<DescriptionListTerm>{Msg.localize('applicationDetailsConsent')}</DescriptionListTerm>

Key to be decided

### Version

18.0.2

### Expected behavior

Localization support should be available for ALL visible texts on the account pages.

### Actual behavior

Some texts remain in English, regardless the support for localization.

### How to Reproduce?

Set the language to something else then english and you'll see some texts will always remain in english as there is no key to be set.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: IdentityProviderModel from third party packages are ignored
issue_body:
### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

Currently, when implementing an extension for Identity Providers, `IdentityProviderModel.getDisplayIconClasses` can be overridden to provide IDP-specific CSS-Classes for the login page icon.

Unfortunately, the override is not considered and the icon is missing on the login page.

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

`IdentityProviderModel.getDisplayIconClasses` is considered when it's overridden in a third party provider package, which enables a custom icon to be shown on the login page.

![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/78362353/201546581-d1973725-3a21-4812-bdac-1cf0efa10c19.png)

### Actual behavior

Overridden implementations of `IdentityProviderModel.getDisplayIconClasses` should be considered, in order to show the respective icon on the login page.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Dowload version `1.2.0` from https://github.com/klausbetz/apple-identity-provider-keycloak
2. Add the package to the provider folder of keycloak.
3. Start keycloak
4. Configure Apple Identity Provider with dummy values and enable it
5. Navigate to the login page

### Anything else?

https://github.com/klausbetz/apple-identity-provider-keycloak/issues/10
commit_message: fix: consider identity provider models from third-party packages

issue_title: Unable to run map-storage-jpa tests with custom Postgres image
issue_body:
### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

According to the [documentation](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/testsuite/integration-arquillian/HOW-TO-RUN.md#running-tests-with-jpa-map-storage), it is possible to run JPA Map storage tests using a custom Postgres image defined in system property `keycloak.map.storage.postgres.docker.image`.

### Version

19.0.0

### Expected behavior

The custom Postgres image is pulled and tests are executed.

### Actual behavior

The custom image is not pulled and produces the following error:

`Caused by: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Failed to verify that image 'MY_IMAGE' is a compatible substitute for 'postgres'. This generally means that you are trying to use an image that Testcontainers has not been designed to use. If this is deliberate, and if you are confident that the image is compatible, you should declare compatibility in code using the `asCompatibleSubstituteFor` method. For example:
2022-11-25 11:52:46     DockerImageName myImage = DockerImageName.parse("MY_IMAGE").asCompatibleSubstituteFor("postgres");
2022-11-25 11:52:46  and then use `myImage` instead.`

### How to Reproduce?

1. `mvn clean install -DskipTests`
2. `mvn -f testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/pom.xml clean install -Pauth-server-undertow -Pmap-storage,map-storage-jpa,db-postgres -Dpageload.timeout=90000 -Dkeycloak.map.storage.postgres.docker.image=MY_IMAGE`

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: clientID are base64 encoded in state uri param with non URI friendly base64
issue_body:
### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

Hi,

Since #10227 some clientID (in "state") are Base64 encoded but not in URI friendly form despite the warning in the pull request.

New code in IdentityBrokerState.

This new code use java.util.Base64 and can produce non URL safe parameter.
It produce url paramater "state" with + or /.
Sample : c5ac1ea7-6c28-4be1-b7cd-d63a1ba57f78 >  xawep2woS+G3zdY6G6V/eA

The "+" seems well encoded in url with %2B but some js adapter decode the complete uri and remplace %2B with "+".

"+" in URI is a "space".

The callback url is then like this with "+" instead of "%2B"

https://{host}/auth/realms/{realmName}/broker/{broker-name}/endpoint?state=EeW2DGPPk6sdZG6jgymATsRqZeNpj3iy6lP1QAMATnc.uJpIOdQ89-w.xawep2woS+G3zdY6G6V%2FeA&session_state={session}&code={code}

When state is decoded, the "+" is interpreted as "space" and the session is not found.

DEBUG [org.keycloak.protocol.RestartLoginCookie] (executor-thread-3) Skip restarting from the KC_RESTART. Clients doesn't match: Cookie client: cityzen.app.crm_frontend, Requested client: xawep2woS G3zdY6G6V/eA

In the original PR #10231 @ahus1 as already warn about : 
> The alphabet used by Base64 is not URL safe

@mposolda say 
>We have Base64Url class inside the codebase, which effectively does this. So hopefully using that class should be ok
..
>Please use org.keycloak.common.util.Base64Url to encode/decode to avoid reinventing the wheel for convert into URL friendly format.

But the code merged use :
(java.util.)Base64.getEncoder()
(java.util.)Base64.getDecoder()

The original PR was about to reduce the length of RelayState.
Using BASE64Url don't increase length

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Base64#URL_applications
>using [URL encoders/decoders](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Percent-encoding) is no longer necessary and **has no effect on the length of the encoded value**

I propose to use standard java.util.Base64.getUrlDecoder() and Base64.getUrlEncoder() instead or the internal
org.keycloak.common.util.Base64Url

Thanks

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Don't encode clientID with "+" ou "/" in URI

Encode c5ac1ea7-6c28-4be1-b7cd-d63a1ba57f78 as Base64URI Friendly : "xawep2woS-G3zdY6G6V_eA"

### Actual behavior

c5ac1ea7-6c28-4be1-b7cd-d63a1ba57f78 is encoded as "xawep2woS+G3zdY6G6V/eA" in URI




### How to Reproduce?

Uuid wich is encoded with "+"  : c5ac1ea7-6c28-4be1-b7cd-d63a1ba57f78


### Anything else?

#10231
#10227
commit_message:
Use org.keycloak.common.util.Base64Url to encode/decode clientID

    fix #15734
    related #10227 #10231
